meta.title: Aethyr for Unreal Engine 5: AI that speaks Blueprint
meta.description: A corruption-safe MCP server that lets any MCP client read and author Unreal Engine 5 Blueprints in plain language. 82 proven tools. No editor scripting.

title: AI THAT SPEAKS
BLUEPRINT
hero.sub: Point your AI assistant at your project and just ask. It reads and rewrites your Unreal Engine 5 Blueprints in plain language, with no editor scripting, no C++, and nothing that can corrupt your assets.

features.heading:
An arsenal your AI
can actually wield.

cards.title: See everything
cards.body: Variables, functions, graphs, nodes, components, interfaces, timelines: your AI reads the whole structure of a Blueprint, no guessing.

cards.title: Author by asking
cards.body: “Add a dash ability with a 2-second cooldown.” It spawns the variables, wires the nodes, and compiles—the way you'd do it by hand, but spoken.

cards.title: Corruption-safe by design
cards.body: Every write saves through a compile gate and an automatic backup ring. Anything that could damage a project simply isn't in this build.

cards.title: All-or-nothing batches
cards.body: Chain a dozen edits into one transaction. If step seven fails, the whole thing rolls back; your graph is never left half-rewritten.

cards.title: Your AI, your client
cards.body: Speaks the Model Context Protocol, so it works with any MCP client, Claude included. Point it at a .uproject and go.

cards.title: Runs in the dark
cards.body: Drives a headless editor or talks to a live one over a socket, auto-routing per call. No babysitting an editor window required.

snippet.heading: Paste and go.
snippet.caption: Add to your MCP client config. Replace the path with wherever you unpacked the plugin.

steps.title: Bind your AI
steps.body: Drop the server into your MCP client's config. One JSON block — paste, point at your project, done.

steps.title: Speak the intent
steps.body: Ask in plain language. “What does BP_Enemy do?” “Refactor this into a function.” “Give the player a double-jump.”

steps.title: Watch it weave
steps.body: It reads, edits, compiles, and saves: atomically, backed up, on the real asset. You review the diff.

items.a: No. Every write goes through a compile gate: if the Blueprint doesn't compile, the save is aborted. Before anything changes, an automatic backup copy is made. Reads are entirely zero-write—nothing on disk changes.

items.q: Which MCP clients does it support?
items.a: Any client that speaks the stdio MCP transport—Claude (Anthropic), Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf, Cline, and others. If your client can load an MCP server, Aethyr works with it.

items.q: Does it need the Unreal Editor open?
items.a: No. Aethyr auto-routes each call: if an editor is running on the project it connects to the live session; otherwise it drives a headless editor automatically. No babysitting an editor window required.
UE 5.4 through 5.8 are verified against real projects. UE 5.3 is untested. UE 4.x and 5.0–5.2 are not supported.

items.q: Does it send data to the cloud?
items.a: No telemetry, no analytics, no cloud dependency. Everything runs on your machine. Your project never leaves your local environment.
Yes. By default Aethyr only reads. Write operations are gated behind the AETHYR_ALLOW_WRITE=1 environment variable. Set it only when you want edits to happen.

summon.heading:
One package.
The proven core.
summon.body: A single, focused build with only the tools we'd stake our reputation on, every one verified against real projects.
summon.fineprint: Free forever · no telemetry · local only

footer.tagline: Made for builders who'd rather describe a mechanic than wire it by hand.
footer.legal: Unreal Engine is a trademark of Epic Games, Inc., referenced for compatibility only. All artwork original.
